Title: Transport of phenoxyacetic acid herbicides by PIN-FORMED auxin transporters

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 2,4-dichlorophenoxyacetic acid; 4-chlorophenoxyacetic acid; phenoxyacetic acid herbicides; indole-3-acetic acid
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin activity controlling plant growth and development; herbicidal action of synthetic auxins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIN8 directly transports phenoxyacetic acid herbicides and endogenous auxin across membranes; mediates NPA-sensitive export of 2,4-D and 4-CPA.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified AtPIN8 binds and transports 2,4-D with affinity and current responses similar to IAA. 2. AtPIN8 also transports low-affinity substrate 4-CPA and cryo-EM captured five transport-cycle states with 4-CPA. 3. Structure-guided mutagenesis identified residues controlling substrate recognition and selectivity for halogenated auxins.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SSM electrophysiology; Xenopus oocyte efflux assay; cryo-EM structural analysis; site-directed mutagenesis; molecular dynamics simulation
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PID (functional activation tested for PIN3, not PIN8); NPA competitively inhibits substrate binding
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PIN-FORMED auxin efflux transporter; transmembrane transporter with 10 TM helices and long cytoplasmic loop; dimeric elevator-type transporter
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous auxin herbicides 2,4-D and 4-CPA; NPA inhibitor
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cryo-EM structures show asynchronous protomer conformations and multiple substrate-bound/release states. Core residues affecting substrate recognition include N117, S146, Y150, N223, S55 and S266.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Structure-guided engineering of PIN transport specificity could be used to create herbicide-resistant crops or alter herbicide mobility.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential alteration of endogenous auxin transport and associated development if engineered; not directly tested.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin/herbicide transport relevant to distribution in plants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIN3 exports 2,4-D in an NPA-sensitive manner and shows enhanced activity with PID kinase; also exports 4-CPA in oocytes.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPIN3 mediates 2,4-D export in Xenopus oocytes. 2. PID kinase activates PIN3-dependent export. 3. PIN3 supports the conclusion that phenoxyacetic acid herbicides are PIN substrates beyond PIN8.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Xenopus oocyte radiotracer efflux assay with and without PID; NPA inhibition
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as family-level corroboration rather than the principal structural subject.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports targeting PIN family transporters to modify herbicide movement.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely affects endogenous auxin transport if altered; not directly tested.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Basipetal auxin and herbicide transport in stem tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIN1 is required for 2,4-D transport in Arabidopsis stem tissue; pin1 mutation abolishes detectable 2,4-D transport in planta.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Stem segment assays show 2,4-D transport in wild type is much lower than IAA but detectable. 2. pin1 mutants abolish 2,4-D transport in inflorescence stems. 3. This demonstrates that in planta 2,4-D transport is PIN-mediated.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis stem transport assay using radiolabeled IAA and 2,4-D; mutant comparison; NPA inhibition
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Inflorescence stem-associated transporter according to assay context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NPA inhibitor; stem transport assay conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed via pin1 mutant as the transporter responsible for auxin transport in this organ; not the main structural focus.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter herbicide translocation in crops.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong developmental defects expected from perturbing PIN1-mediated auxin transport.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin/herbicide distribution through activation of PIN-mediated export
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Activation of transporter function by kinase-dependent regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PID enhances PIN3-mediated export of 2,4-D and IAA in oocytes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results directly test PID coexpression as an activator of PIN3 transport activity, but it is not the main paper focus.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression of PID activates PIN3 transport activity in oocytes. 2. PID increases PIN3-mediated 2,4-D export similarly to its activation of IAA export. 3. The data support kinase control of herbicide transport through PIN regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulation of PIN-activating kinases may tune herbicide movement, though specificity and developmental effects are concerns.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad developmental effects through auxin transport regulation; not directly tested.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coexpression in Xenopus oocytes; NPA inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent activator of PIN3-mediated herbicide export
